LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS ACQUIRED THEIR SHORT-TERM RENTAL BUSINESSES FROM A PERSON WHOM THEY KNEW HAD HIS ACCOUNTS TERMINATED BY AIRBNB FOR ALLEGED VIOLATIONS OF AIRBNB RULES YET FAILED TO NOTIFY AIRBNB THAT THEY WERE, AS THEY CONTEND, UNAFFILIATED WITH THE PRIOR ACCOUNT HOLDERS AND ACTING IN A WAY THAT WAS NOT HARMFUL TO AIRBNB OR OTHERS. AT THE TIME AIRBNB SUSPENDED PLAINTIFFS' ACCOUNTS AND CANCELED BOOKINGS MADE WITH PLAINTIFFS, AIRBNB HAD A GOOD FAITH AND REASONABLE BELIEF THAT PLAINTIFFS WERE AFFILIATED WITH THE TERMINATED ACCOUNT HOLDERS AND THUS AIRBNB HAD A CONTRACTUAL RIGHT TO SUSPEND PLAINTIFFS' ACCOUNTS AND CANCEL THEIR BOOKINGS WITHOUT NOTICE TO PLAINTIFFS. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THEY WOULD SUFFER SIGNIFICANT OR IRREPARABLE HARM IF THEIR REQUESTED INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY DEFENSE. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.(Per signed order Mar-24-2025)
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
1:30pm (Hearing held via Zoom)
Appearances (by Zoom):
Raymond Brenneman, Esq. 310-870-8088 Brenneman APC for PLaintiff
Suzy Orza, Esq. Kajsa Minor, Esq. 415-773-7319 Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant airbnb

Argued. The Court comments. Mr. Brenneman contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

Plaintiffs' motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiffs acquired their short-term rental businesses from a person whom they knew had his accounts terminated by Airbnb for alleged violations of Airbnb rules yet failed to notify Airbnb that they were, as they contend, unaffiliated with the prior account holders and acting in a way that was not harmful to Airbnb or others. At the time Airbnb suspended plaintiffs' accounts and canceled bookings made with plaintiffs, Airbnb had a good faith and reasonable belief that plaintiffs were affiliated with the terminated account holders and thus Airbnb had a contractual right to suspend plaintiffs' accounts and cancel their bookings without notice to plaintiffs. Moreover, plaintiffs have not shown that they would suffer significant or irreparable harm if their requested injunctive relief is denied.

The Court will sign the proposed order submitted by Defense.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Appearances (by Zoom):
Raymond Brenneman, Esq. 310-870-8088 Brenneman APC for Plaintiff.
Kajsa Minor, Esq. Suzy Orza, Esq. 415-773-7319 Shartsis Freise for Defendant Airbnb.

Argued. Both sides present arguments on Plaintiffs' motion for a preliminary injunction.
2:45 Court orders parties to meet and confer re further evidence/ briefing and return to court by 3:15pm.
3:15pm Parties apprise the Court of their agreed upon briefing schedule/submission dates of 4/29, 5/2 and 5/5/25. Parties to email their papers instantaneously to the court via email to his gmail address. Parties stipulate to the appearance of the court remotely via Zoom.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

The motion is continued to 5/7/25 at 1:30pm (via Zoom) before the Hon. Judge Kahn for further hearing.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/hek)
